f,ear Us. Breeze:
The PERS Board of Administration has alvrays been mindful of the
Fair Political Practices Act and other conflict of interest 1aws.
In other continuing effort to avoid even the slightest appearance
of a conflict of interest, the System has been working to develop
a wri.tten policy statement regarding Board and staff travel.
one of our prinary concerns is that the PERS travel policy: 
(1)
be in cornpliance with FPPC statutes/ regui.at ions regarding the

frorn
reportable sources; and (2) provide clear notice of the

reportable sources.
Last month vrhen we spoke by telephone, you graciously agreed to

Your assistance in this regard is deeply appreciated, especialJ-y
in liqht of your heavy workload.
we have enclosed t$/o policy statenents. The first draft policy
statenent is dated f.fay 1992 and is seven pages in length.
The second draft j.s dated June 29, 1992, and is six pages in
Iength.
As you will note, these drafts are different fron each other, but
each attenpts to acconplj-sh the goals set forth above in regard
to the FPPC lalrs.
Calltornla Publlc Employees' Retirement System
t

July 8, 1992
Please let us know if one statenent more accurately reflects the
FPPC laws/ regulations or if both (or neither) accurately reflect
these laws/regulations.
Specifically, in regard to the llay 1992 draft, is the chart on
page 3 of the policy in accordance with coverrunent Code sectj,on
89506? Is it in accordance r.rith other FPPC statutes and
regulations? Are page 5 (paragraphs 2-5) and 6 in accordance
with Governnent Code section 89506? Are they j.n accordance with
other FPPC statutes and regulations?
Specifically, in regard to the June 1992 draft, is the use of the
tern "discussantrr on page 3 at footnote 2, Iine 3, in accordance
with FPPC statutes/ regulations?
Does paragraph F on page 4 raise any concerns regarding gifts or
disqua l if ication under FPPC lae? Does it nake a difference that

reso 1ut i on?
Do the paragraphs on pages 5-5 accurately reflect the FPPC

interstate and foreign travel?
If you bel j.eve that portions of either statement do not
accurately reflect the FPPC Iarrs / regulat ions regarding
gifts/travel 
we rrould appreciate your conments. These draft
policy statements wilL be discussed at the July 28, L992 Benefits
and Progran Administration Connittee meeting and we are hoping to
have your input prior to that meeting if it is possible. I can

very truly yours,
MJH : bb

sections.question: Please let us know if one statement more accurately reflects the FPPC laws/regulations or if both (or neither) accurately reflect these laws/regulations. Specifically, in regard to the May 1992 draft, is the chart on page 3 of the policy in accordance with Government Code section 89506? Is it in accordance with other FPPC statutes and regulations? Are page 5 (paragraphs 2-5) and 6 in accordance with Government Code section 89506? Are they in accordance with other FPPC statutes and regulations? Specifically, in regard to the June 1992 draft, is the use of the term "discussant" on page 3 at footnote 2, line 3, in accordance with FPPC statutes/regulations? Does paragraph F on page 4 raise any concerns regarding gifts or disqualification under FPPC law? Does it make a difference that PERS has no gift policy and has never accepted gifts by resolution? Do the paragraphs on pages 5-6 accurately reflect the FPPC statutes/regulations regarding acceptance of gifts of intrastate, interstate and foreign travel?
sections.question_synthetic: The California Public Employees' Retirement System (PERS) seeks informal FPPC review of two draft travel policy statements to determine whether they accurately comply with FPPC statutes and regulations regarding limitations and prohibitions on accepting gifts and travel from reportable sources.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to FPPC, not an FPPC response. The document contains multiple specific questions about two draft policy statements but no formal FPPC conclusion or advice. The letter references an informal telephone agreement with Ms. Breeze to review the policies.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Please let us know if one statement more accurately reflects the FPPC laws/regulations or if both (or neither) accurately reflect these laws/regulations. Specifically, in regard to the May 1992 draft, is the chart on page 3 of the policy in accordance with Government Code section 89506? Is it in accordance with other FPPC statutes and regulations? Are page 5 (paragraphs 2-5) and 6 in accordance with Government Code section 89506? Are they in accordance with other FPPC statutes and regulations? Specifically, in regard to the June 1992 draft, is the use of the term "discussant" on page 3 at footnote 2, line 3, in accordance with FPPC statutes/regulations? Does paragraph F on page 4 raise any concerns regarding gifts or disqualification under FPPC law? Does it make a difference that PERS has no gift policy and has never accepted gifts by resolution? Do the paragraphs on pages 5-6 accurately reflect the FPPC statutes/regulations regarding acceptance of gifts of intrastate, interstate and foreign travel?

embedding.summary: The PERS Board of Administration requests informal FPPC review of two draft travel policy statements to ensure compliance with conflict of interest laws, specifically regarding the acceptance of gifts and travel from reportable sources under Government Code section 89506.
